story.asA: user considering Recipe for preset conversion
story.iWant: a clear legal disclaimer visible on the landing page explaining reverse engineering disclosure, no-warranty terms, and private use recommendations
story.soThat: I understand the legal landscape and can make an informed decision about using Recipe while the developer maintains transparency about limitations and legal compliance

tasks.task: Draft Legal Disclaimer Content (AC-1, AC-2, AC-3, AC-5)
Update README.md with Legal Disclaimer Link

ac.title: Disclaimer Includes Reverse Engineering Disclosure
ac.details: Reverse engineering stated clearly (not hidden or implied) - Specific formats mentioned (Nikon .np3) - Vendor non-affiliation stated explicitly - Legal basis referenced (fair use, DMCA exemptions) - Tone transparent and matter-of-fact

ac.title: No-Warranty Statement is Present
ac.details: Standard "AS IS" disclaimer using MIT License or similar language - States software provided "as is" without warranties - No guarantees about conversion accuracy or compatibility - Limitation of liability for data loss or damages - User assumes all risk of use

ac.title: Recommends Private Use
ac.details: Private/personal use explicitly recommended - Commercial use requires legal consultation - Rationale explained (legal landscape varies, no comprehensive review) - Purchased presets copyright respected - Tone balanced (not overly restrictive)

ac.title: Disclaimer is Visible on Landing Page
ac.details: Placed in footer section or dedicated section - Visible without clicks (not hidden in accordion) - Clear heading ("Legal Disclaimer") - Anchor ID for deep linking (#legal-disclaimer) - Readable font size

ac.title: Legally Reviewed (If Possible) or Conservative Template Used
ac.details: For MVP: Use conservative MIT License template - Adapt template to Recipe context (reverse engineering) - Document lack of legal review (transparency) - Note legal review may be pursued post-launch - "Last Updated" date present

doc.title: Product Requirements Document
doc.snippet: Requirement: Disclose reverse engineering and limitations. Content: Reverse engineering disclosure, No warranty statement, Private use recommendation (until legal assessment). Acceptance Criteria: Visible on landing page, Legally reviewed (if possible), User acknowledges before use (optional).

doc.title: Product Requirements Document
doc.snippet: The Problem: Nikon uses proprietary .np3 Picture Control files for in-camera color profiles, while the photography community creates and shares presets in Adobe Lightroom formats. No converter exists, and Nikon's .np3 format is undocumented. Reverse engineering achievement: Cracked Nikon's undocumented .np3 binary format. Legal Considerations: Reverse engineering for interoperability (likely legal in most jurisdictions). Private repository initially to assess legal risk.

doc.title: Epic 7 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: AC-1: Disclaimer includes reverse engineering disclosure. AC-2: No-warranty statement is present. AC-3: Recommends private use. AC-4: Disclaimer visible on landing page. AC-5: Legally reviewed or conservative template used. Legal disclaimer covering reverse engineering disclosure and no-warranty statement.

doc.title: Architecture Document
doc.snippet: Recipe is a universal photo preset converter built with a privacy-first, client-side architecture. Key Architectural Characteristics: Privacy: Zero server uploads, all processing client-side (Web) or local (CLI/TUI). Technology Foundation: Go 1.24+, Vanilla JavaScript (Web interface, no frameworks), Cloudflare Pages (static hosting for Web interface).

doc.title: Story 7-3: FAQ Documentation
doc.snippet: FAQ answers "Is Recipe legal? (reverse engineering)" - Explains reverse engineering context, fair use doctrine, and private use recommendation. FAQ should link to formal Legal Disclaimer for detailed terms.

doc.title: Story 7-1: Landing Page
doc.snippet: Landing page provides location for disclaimer section. Section structure, CSS styling patterns, footer link structure can be reused for legal disclaimer integration.

doc.title: Story 7-2: Format Compatibility Matrix
doc.snippet: Technical transparency: 95%+ accuracy target, format limitations documented. Legal protection: No guarantee of accuracy, use at own risk. Together: Transparency + Protection (honest about capabilities, protected from liability).

artifact.reason: Current landing page structure - legal disclaimer will be added as new section before footer. Footer already exists with links structure that can be extended.
Example of existing FAQ-style content structure with toggle functionality. Legal disclaimer will use similar section structure but without toggle (always visible per AC-4).
Footer contains existing links and disclaimer text. Will be extended to add link to #legal-disclaimer anchor and updated privacy disclaimer text.

constraints.constraint: No external CSS frameworks - use vanilla CSS with custom properties (consistent with Epic 2 web interface design).
Legal disclaimer must be visible without requiring clicks (AC-4) - no accordion/toggle unlike Privacy FAQ.
Use conservative MIT License warranty template for MVP - comprehensive legal review not required (AC-5).
Maintain consistency with FAQ legal answer (Story 7-3) - same legal basis (Fair Use, DMCA 1201), private use recommendation.
Privacy-first architecture - disclaimer reinforces zero tracking, no analytics, no data collection (aligns with FR-2.9).
Mobile-responsive design - legal text must be readable on mobile devices (no horizontal scroll, appropriate font size).
Anchor ID required - #legal-disclaimer for deep linking from README, FAQ, and footer navigation.

interface.name: Landing Page HTML Structure
interface.signature:
⚖️ Legal Disclaimer

...

Reverse Engineering Disclosure

...

No Warranty

...

Recommended Use

...

About This Disclaimer

...

interface.name: CSS Styling for Legal Section

tests.standards: Manual testing approach for documentation story. No automated tests required (content-focused story). Visual verification and link testing will be performed via browser DevTools and manual inspection.
tests.locations: Manual testing checklist in story document (Task 7). No test files created for this story.

ideas.idea: Manual review: Search landing page HTML for "reverse-engineered" text and verify Nikon .np3 format mentioned, legal basis cited (Fair Use, DMCA), vendor non-affiliation stated.
Manual review: Search for "AS IS" or "no warranty" text in ALL CAPS. Verify limitation of liability, user assumes risk.
Manual review: Verify private/personal use recommended, commercial use requires attorney consultation, preset copyright respected.
Browser test: Scroll to legal disclaimer section, verify visible without clicks. Test footer link jumps to #legal-disclaimer. Test deep link: https://recipe.pages.dev/#legal-disclaimer
Manual review: Verify "About This Disclaimer" section documents MIT License base, legal review status (not completed), "Last Updated" date present.
Mobile responsiveness test: View on mobile device or browser DevTools mobile view. Verify legal text readable (not tiny font), no horizontal scroll, sections stack vertically.
Consistency check: Cross-reference with FAQ legal answer (Story 7-3) and format compatibility matrix (Story 7-2). Verify same legal basis, private use recommendation, no contradictions.
